mundane_daily_life: The water level's a bit lower than it was last week.
There's a crack in that wall that wasn't there yesterday. Or maybe it was.
One of the stones on that ledge is loose. I keep meaning to fix it.
Smells like wet clay out here. Always does around this time.
I had bread and salt for lunch. Same as yesterday.
The door sticks when it's humid. Today it stuck twice.
I can hear the water from here if I stand in the right spot.
Somebody left a crate by the wall. Been there since last week.
The mortar between those stones is crumbling. You can see it from here.
There's a puddle that never dries up over by the ridge. Strange spot for it.
I keep finding the same kind of rock in my garden: small, round, grey.
The lantern oil's burning faster than it should. Might be the wick.
Something's been digging holes near the old foundation. Small ones.
The hinges on that gate squeak. I oiled them two days ago.
I swept the front step three times today. Dust keeps coming back.
The old post out there leans a little more every season.
There's a draft coming from somewhere and I cannot figure out where.
Woke up with a rock in my boot. Don't even remember walking through gravel.
I set out a jar of preserves last night and something got into it.
I've been waiting on a delivery for six days now. Six.
Bit into a pear and found half a worm. Would've preferred a whole one, honestly.
The lid on this crate hasn't fit right since I bought it.
I had the same thing for breakfast three days running.
There's a puddle by the front step that never dries. Never.
My back's been acting up since I moved that barrel. Wasn't even a heavy one.
There's a creak in the floor right where I stand most of the day.
Smoke keeps blowing back down the chimney. The whole room smells like ash.
I organized that shelf yesterday. Already a mess.
Got soup on my sleeve this morning and I only have the one clean shirt.
The hook by the door fell out of the wall again. Took a chunk of plaster with it.
Was the sky this color yesterday? I feel like it wasn't.
What did I come out here for again...
I swear that rock used to be on the other side of the path.
I had something important to remember: now what was it.
The wind changed direction three times today. That's unusual... I think.
There's a word for that feeling when you forget why you stood up. I forget what it is.
I keep counting the stones in that wall and getting a different number.
Someone should really oil that gate. Not me, but someone.
The creek sounds different at night. Louder, maybe. Or just... different.
If I plant something here, would it grow? The soil looks alright.
I've been staring at that cloud for a while now. It hasn't moved.
Funny how a path just appears where enough people walk.
I thought I left the lantern burning. Or did I blow it out? I should go check... later.
The old tower looks shorter than it used to. Do towers shrink?
I keep thinking today is market day: it's not market day.
How long has that door been hanging crooked? Surely I would have noticed.
I've been standing on a pebble for the last few minutes. Just noticed.
Something dripped on my shoulder. Looked up: nothing there.
I just counted the stones in that wall. Lost count. Now I have to start over.
There was a sound from behind that door. Like a tap. Just one.
I set my pack down and it fell over. Twice. Same spot.
A leaf just blew past going the wrong direction. Wind's coming from the east.
I bit my tongue mid-thought. Completely lost what I was thinking about.
One of my buttons came loose. Not off, just: loose. It's going to bother me all day.
I went to lean on that railing and it wobbled. Not broken, just: wobbled.
I dropped a coin earlier and it rolled under something. Still thinking about it.
My left boot has been squeaking since the bridge. Only the left one.
I yawned so wide just now my jaw clicked. Never happened before.
I forgot what I came outside for. Stood here long enough that it stopped mattering.
I just pulled a splinter out of my palm. Don't remember touching any wood today.
My water skin's been dripping. Checked the seal three times: it's fine.
My left knee does this thing when I stand too long.
I could eat. Not starving, just: I could eat.
I've been sitting here so long my foot went numb.
Something on my back itches and I can't quite reach it.
I bit my tongue earlier and now I can't stop poking it.
My shoulder's been doing this clicking thing all morning.
I should've eaten something before I came out here.
My hands are freezing. Been clenching them all day.
I yawned so hard a minute ago my jaw still aches.
There's a rock in my boot. Been there an hour. Haven't dealt with it.
My neck's so stiff I have to turn my whole body to look at anything.
I've got this knot in my calf that won't go away.
My eyes are doing that thing where they won't stop watering.
Haven't sat down since sunrise. My heels are letting me know.
I keep almost sneezing. It won't commit.
My arm fell asleep and now it's doing the pins and needles.
I'm not tired, exactly. My legs are tired. The rest of me is fine.
I'd give a lot for a cold drink right now. Anything, really.
My lower back just started up again. Happens every afternoon.
I can feel my own heartbeat in my fingertips. That's probably fine.
One of my eyelids has been twitching since yesterday.
I slept on my arm wrong and it still doesn't feel like mine.
My throat's dry. I keep swallowing and it doesn't help.
I'm warm in all the wrong places and cold in all the rest.
My hip pops every time I shift my weight. Listen: there it goes.
I need to check the water barrel before dark.
After this I've got to go see about that thing by the gate.
Still have to patch that section of wall before it rains.
I told myself I'd fix that hinge today. We'll see.
One more trip to the well and I'm done for the evening.
I've been putting off sorting the stockroom all week.
Just got back from the ridge: the path up there is getting worse.
I should probably eat something before I head out again.
Three more crates to move, then I can sit down.
The old bridge needs looking at. I'll get to it tomorrow. Probably.
I keep meaning to oil that gate: it's been squealing for days.
Left my tools by the stream. Going to regret that if it floods.
I promised I'd bring firewood up before sundown. Running out of sundown.
Just counted the stores: we're lower on salt than I thought.
I was halfway to the crossing before I realized I forgot the rope.
Someone moved my ladder. Now I have to find my ladder.
Got the morning rounds done early for once. Not sure what to do with myself.
That loose stone on the footpath isn't going to fix itself. Unfortunately.
I need to return this bucket to the hall and then I'm free.
Been meaning to clear the brush along the lower wall all month.
The lanterns need filling tonight: I keep telling myself I'll do it early.
I carried water up that hill twice already today. Might be a third time.
I said I'd check the cellar stores an hour ago. Still standing here.
weather: Wind's been coming from the same direction for three days now.
I found a beetle on my windowsill this morning. Just sat there.
The path's muddier than usual. Not sure when it rained.
I left a cup outside and it was full of rainwater by morning. Didn't hear a drop.
Smoke from the chimney's going straight up today. No wind at all.
The shadows move differently when there's no wind. I keep noticing that.
craftsmanship: I've asked three times about getting that hinge fixed. Three times.
I keep finding splinters in this table. Sanded it twice already.
The latch on my gate sticks when it's damp out. So: always.
I left my good knife somewhere and I've been using this dull one all week.
The stones on this path are laid wrong. You can feel it underfoot.
I think about that well every single morning. The stonework is flawless.
That rope's been tied to that ring since before the bridge was repaired. Good knot.
There's a nail in that beam that's worked itself halfway out. Bothers me.
That ladder leans two degrees too far to the left. I've checked.
Someone carved a notch in that post. I want to know why.
The hinge on that shutter squeaks, but only when the wind comes from the east.
I've never liked that arch. The keystone sits too low.
That torch holder is mounted at exactly the right height. Most aren't.
The water trough by the road: someone carved a little fish on the inside. Beautiful work.
community: Someone moved my stool. I had it exactly where I wanted it.
My neighbor hums. All day. The same four notes.
Someone keeps stacking crates in front of my door. I have to move them every morning.
The candles they sell now burn down twice as fast as they used to.
They changed the bread recipe and didn't tell anyone. I can taste the difference.
That post has been here longer than I have. I respect it.
I don't trust that gate. Never have.
This is a good wall. Solid.
See that barrel? It's the only one around here that doesn't leak.
Someone left this crate here years ago. I've grown attached to it.
That fence has no business being as sturdy as it is.
I've been watching that door. It doesn't close right: never has.
There's a crack in that step. I think about it more than I should.
That bucket is the wrong size. I can't explain it, but I know it.
Whoever hung that lantern knew what they were doing.
I've sat on every bench within a day's walk. This one's the best.
That sign used to say something. I liked it better when it did.
This ledge right here: perfect height for setting things down. Perfect.
There used to be a second hook on that wall. I miss it.
That pile of bricks has been there for three seasons. No one's claimed them.
trade: The bread was stale this morning. Again.
curiosity: I wonder if the well always sounded like that.
I wonder who built that bridge. Must have taken ages.
If you patch a fence enough times, is it still the same fence?
I had a dream about this exact spot last night. Or maybe the night before.
My lantern flickered a moment ago. Oil's full. No wind. Can't explain it.
There's a crack in this wall that I swear wasn't here last week.
I watched a stone roll off that ledge on its own. No wind, no one near it.
nature: There's a bird that sits on that post every morning. Hasn't shown up today.
There's a root pushing up one of the flagstones on the path.
Mushrooms came up overnight by the north wall. Brownish ones.
Do birds ever get tired of singing the same song? I would.
That's the third crow today. Same one, maybe. Hard to tell.
I wonder if fish know when it's about to rain.
Something smells like rosemary. I don't remember planting rosemary.
The moss only grows on one side. I can never remember which side that's supposed to mean.
A bird just landed on that post, looked right at me, and left. No idea what that was about.
A beetle walked across my boot. Took its time, too.
I sneezed a moment ago and scared a lizard off the fence. Feel terrible about it.
I just watched a spider rebuild its web after I walked through it. Felt like an apology was owed.
A frog just appeared on that rock. Wasn't there a second ago. I was looking right at it.
Something brushed past my ankle in the tall grass. Probably nothing. Probably.
A moth flew into my ear. Brief visit. Still recovering.
nostalgia: A good path doesn't need to be straight. Think about that.
There's no such thing as a small leak. Every leak is a big leak eventually.
You can tell a lot about a place by its fences.
I've been thinking about buckets. A bucket with a hole isn't a bucket: it's a lesson.
Soup is just patience in a bowl. Most people don't understand that.
The thing about doors is: every single one of them is a decision.
I used to rush through lunch. Took me years to realize lunch was the only honest part of the day.
A wall that leans is still a wall. That's worth remembering.
Nobody talks about how a good nap can change your entire position on something.
I've carried a lot of things in my life. The heaviest was always the second trip.
You ever notice how water always finds the lowest point? Never the easiest one: the lowest.
There are two kinds of rope: rope you trust, and rope you haven't tested yet.
A chair with three legs will teach you more about balance than any book.
I spent a whole afternoon watching a bridge once. It didn't go anywhere, but it meant something.
The best firewood is the kind you stacked yesterday. That's not about firewood: that's about everything.
People underestimate porridge. Porridge has never let anyone down.
A gate that swings both ways isn't indecisive. It's generous.
You can patch a roof or you can think about patching a roof. Only one of those keeps you dry.
I've never met a stone that was in a hurry. There's something to that.
The difference between a path and a rut is just how you feel about where it's going.
Bread rises when it's ready, not when you want it to. Most important thing I ever learned.
An old ladder still knows how high it can reach. That's more than most people can say.
I keep my tools sharp. Not because I need to cut anything: because dull things make you careless.
Rain doesn't care about your plans. That's not cruelty, that's just honesty.
The second-best time to fix a fence is right now. The best time was before it fell.
